evidence: The paper uses "corrected impurity measure which estimates mean decrease in accuracy" via ranger to rank importance of 32 predictors. The core conclusions depend on this ranking: "Location variables, including longitude, latitude and country had high predictive power...Forest landscape characteristics tended to be very important...Factors relating directly to forms of human pressure...had little influence." These importance-based rankings drive policy recommendations that "restoration efforts...should target areas with high surrounding forest landscape integrity." The feature space is vulnerable to impurity bias: data are "heavily skewed towards younger forests" (52% age ≤5 years), include mixed variable types (continuous environmental, high-cardinality categorical locations like country, landscape indices), and lack corroboration beyond partial dependence plots (which show shape, not relative importance).
